intended_use: The Volta AF-Xplorer assists operators in the real-time manual or automatic annotation of 3D anatomical and electrical maps of human atria for the presence of multipolar intra-cardiac atrial electrogr
intended_use_full: The Volta AF-Xplorer assists operators in the real-time manual or automatic annotation of 3D anatomical and electrical maps of human atria for the presence of multipolar intra-cardiac atrial electrograms exhibiting spatiotemporal dispersion during atrial fibrillation or atrial tachycardia.
target_condition: atrial fibrillation and atrial tachycardia, specifically drug-refractory persistent atrial fibrillation
ground_truth: Clinical outcomes including documented atrial fibrillation recurrence assessed via 12-lead electrocardiograms, 24-hour Holter monitoring, and 6-lead Kardia portable monitor with weekly transmissions over 12-month follow-up period.
other_metrics: Primary effectiveness endpoint: 88% freedom from documented atrial fibrillation lasting >30 seconds in Tailored arm (n=158/180) versus 70% in Anatomical arm (n=124/177) at 12 months post-procedure (p<0.0001). Secondary endpoint freedom from any atrial arrhythmia: 76% Tailored arm versus 71% Anatomical arm (not statistically significant). Major procedure-related complications: 2.7% both arms. Minor procedure-related complications: 8.0% Tailored arm versus 3.2% Anatomical arm. Procedure time: 178±60 minutes (Tailored) versus 92±36 minutes (Anatomical). Ablation time: 42±17 minutes (Tailored) versus 20±11 minutes (Anatomical).
